proposed_hypotheses.text: The overall objective_response rate in this 50,000-patient NSCLC cohort is non-trivial (>5%) and the outcome is sufficiently variable to support subsequent subgroup and interaction analyses.

analyses.result_summary: Overall ORR is 0.169 (8447/50000). Outcome prevalence supports subsequent analyses.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 77 binary features, at least one shows a statistically significant association (chi-square p < 0.05) with objective_response in univariate analysis.
feature_013 (prevalence ~65%) is associated with LOWER objective_response (negative risk difference), based on univariate chi-square testing.

analyses.code: for c in binary_cols: chi2_contingency(crosstab(df[c], df.y))
analyses.result_summary: Binary screen identified multiple significant predictors. feature_013: ORR 15.4% if present (n=32365) vs 19.6% if absent (n=17635), risk difference -0.042, OR=0.74, chi-square p=1.1e-33. Direction NEGATIVE as hypothesized.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_067 (prevalence ~25%) is associated with LOWER objective_response (negative risk difference).
feature_006 (prevalence ~29%) is associated with HIGHER objective_response (positive risk difference), suggestive of a treatment-like exposure.

analyses.result_summary: feature_067: ORR 14.4% if present (n=12584) vs 17.7% if absent (n=37416). Risk difference -0.034, OR=0.78, p=2.0e-18. Direction NEGATIVE as hypothesized.
feature_006: ORR 18.2% if present (n=14258) vs 16.4% if absent (n=35742). Risk difference +0.018, OR=1.14, p=1.0e-6. Direction POSITIVE as hypothesized.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_007 (prevalence ~45%) and feature_039 (prevalence ~50%) are each associated with HIGHER objective_response (positive risk differences), consistent with treatment-component features.

analyses.code: chi2 tests for feature_007 and feature_039
analyses.result_summary: feature_007: ORR 17.5% vs 16.4%, RD=+0.012, OR=1.09, p=5.9e-4. feature_039: ORR 17.4% vs 16.4%, RD=+0.010, OR=1.07, p=3.4e-3. Both positive and significant as hypothesized.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_051 (integer 0/1/2, mean ~0.80; structurally consistent with ECOG performance status) is associated with progressively LOWER objective_response as its value increases (linear/monotonic dose-response).

analyses.result_summary: ORR by feature_051 level: 21.1% at 0 (n=17557), 15.7% at 1 (n=24962), 11.1% at 2 (n=7481). Logit slope coef=-0.41 per unit, p<1e-100. Strict monotonic decrease as hypothesized — strongest single predictor in the dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 (continuous, range 0-28, mean 3.84; potentially reflecting number of metastatic sites or prior therapy lines) is associated with LOWER objective_response with a monotonic decline across quintiles.

analyses.result_summary: ORR by feature_011 quintile: Q1 18.9% (0-1.7), Q2 17.0%, Q3 15.8%, Q4-Q5 13.7% (7.2-28). Logit coef=-0.039 per unit, p=2.0e-33. Monotonic dose-response confirmed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_092 (continuous, range 0-0.80, mean 0.31; structurally consistent with PD-L1 tumor proportion score) is positively associated with objective_response in the overall cohort.

analyses.result_summary: feature_092 logit coef=+0.18 per unit (i.e., per 100 percentage points TPS), OR=1.19. p=2.0e-3. Positive as hypothesized. Tertile means: low 16.6%, mid 16.4%, high 17.4%. Modest main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_099 (continuous, range 1.7-5.5, mean 3.80; structurally consistent with serum albumin) is positively associated with objective_response, consistent with albumin's prognostic role.

analyses.result_summary: feature_099 logit coef=+0.094 per unit, OR=1.10, p=1.3e-4. Tertile means: low 16.1%, mid 17.1%, high 17.6%. Positive monotonic relationship as hypothesized.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_063 (continuous, right-skewed, range 0.03-296.88; potentially an inflammatory or tumor-burden marker) is NEGATIVELY associated with objective_response, with monotonic decline across quintiles.

analyses.result_summary: feature_063 logit coef=-0.0050 per unit, p=4.2e-4. Quintile ORR: 18.2%, 17.0%, 16.9%, 16.6%, 15.7%. Monotonic decline confirmed; direction NEGATIVE as hypothesized.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_014 (range 6-18, mean 12.5; consistent with hemoglobin) is positively associated with objective_response.
feature_066 (range 0.29-30.4, mean 3.49; structurally consistent with neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio, NLR) is negatively associated with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: feature_014 coef=-0.0035, p=0.60. Tertile ORR: 17.0%, 16.7%, 17.0%. NULL — no association detected, contrary to hypothesis.
feature_066 coef=-0.0050, p=0.38. Tertile ORR: 16.9%, 17.0%, 16.8%. NULL — no association detected, contrary to hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_078 (continuous, range 30-90, mean 65; clearly age in years) is associated with objective_response, with older patients having LOWER ORR.

analyses.result_summary: feature_078 coef=+0.0005 per year, p=0.67. Quintile ORR: 17.0%, 16.5%, 17.5%, 16.4%, 17.1%. NULL — no monotonic age effect, REFUTING the hypothesis. Age is essentially independent of ORR in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_044 (continuous, mean ~28; potentially BMI) shows a non-monotonic or inverse relationship with objective_response, with the highest tertile having lower ORR.

analyses.result_summary: feature_044 coef=-0.0018, p=0.082. Tertile ORR: 17.1%, 17.4%, 16.3%. Top tertile is lower; trend in the hypothesized direction but not significant at alpha=0.05.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the categorical features, feature_057 (current/former/never smoker), feature_043 (adenocarcinoma/squamous histology), feature_123 (race), and feature_005 (insurance) each show statistically significant association (chi-square p<0.05) with ORR in unadjusted analysis.

analyses.result_summary: feature_057 (smoking): ORR current 17.2%, former 16.8%, never 16.4%, chi2 p=0.27 (NS). feature_043 (histology): adeno 16.7%, squamous 17.4%, p=0.078 (NS). feature_123 (race): Asian 17.7%, Black 17.8%, Hispanic 17.2%, Other 18.5%, White 16.5%, chi2 p=0.045 (significant). feature_005 (insurance): medicaid 16.1%, medicare 16.8%, private 17.2%, uninsured 17.0%, p=0.23 (NS). Hypothesis only partially supported — only race is significant in unadjusted univariate testing.

proposed_hypotheses.text: REFINED: While unadjusted main effects of smoking, histology, and insurance are not significant, a residual ORR difference between Medicaid and Private insurance (Medicaid LOWER) persists after adjusting for clinical features (feature_051 ECOG-like, feature_013, feature_011), implicating a real disparity rather than confounding.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted for clinical features, private vs medicaid coef=+0.087 (OR=1.09, p=0.022). Medicare vs medicaid coef=+0.062 (p=0.10). Private patients retain significantly higher adjusted ORR vs Medicaid; gap is not explained by ECOG/feature_013/feature_011.

proposed_hypotheses.text: REFINED: The marginal racial difference in ORR (favoring non-white groups) DISAPPEARS after adjusting for clinical features, suggesting confounding by ECOG/feature_013/feature_011 distribution rather than a direct race effect.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted for clinical features, all race coefficients vs reference (Asian) are non-significant: Black 0.00, Hispanic -0.04, Other +0.05, White -0.08 (all p>0.11). Racial differences vanish after adjustment, supporting the confounding hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006, feature_007, and feature_039 act ADDITIVELY (and possibly synergistically) on ORR — the more of these three positive features a patient has, the higher their ORR, with a particular jump at all three positive (consistent with combination-regimen exposure indicators).

analyses.result_summary: ORR by sum of (f006+f007+f039): 0/3 = 16.1% (n=9882), 1/3 = 16.4% (n=21815), 2/3 = 16.8% (n=15087), 3/3 = 23.0% (n=3216). Monotonic and a clear jump at 3/3, consistent with combination-regimen interpretation.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pairwise interactions among feature_006, feature_007, and feature_039 are statistically significant POSITIVE (super-additive), meaning their joint effect exceeds the sum of main effects.

analyses.result_summary: All three pairwise interactions positive and significant: f006:f007 coef=+0.145 (p=0.005), f006:f039 coef=+0.180 (p=0.001), f007:f039 coef=+0.142 (p=0.003). Each main effect is essentially zero or slightly negative; benefit emerges only from co-occurrence — strong synergy as hypothesized.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_092 (PD-L1-like) interacts with smoking status (feature_057) such that its positive effect on ORR is restricted to ever-smokers and is REVERSED in never-smokers, consistent with the known biology of immune-checkpoint response (PD-L1 predicts response in smokers but not in never-smokers, who often harbor driver mutations).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction term feature_092:never significant (coef=-0.525, p=0.004). Stratified slopes: current smokers OR=1.21 (p=0.06), former smokers OR=1.33 (p=2e-4 — strong positive), never smokers OR=0.72 (p=0.024 — significantly NEGATIVE). Direction reversal in never-smokers confirmed exactly as hypothesized.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_092 (PD-L1-like) and the combination-regimen indicator (all3 = f006*f007*f039) show a strong POSITIVE synergistic interaction: only the joint presence of high PD-L1 AND all three regimen components produces a substantial ORR benefit (consistent with biomarker-selected immunotherapy combinations).

analyses.result_summary: ORR cells: all3=0/high_pdl1=0 16.5%; all3=0/high_pdl1=1 16.4%; all3=1/high_pdl1=0 17.3%; all3=1/high_pdl1=1 33.2% (n=1148). Interaction coef=+0.876, p<1e-22. Without the regimen, PD-L1 status is irrelevant (16.5% vs 16.4%); without high PD-L1, the regimen barely helps; together, ORR doubles. Striking synergy as hypothesized.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The triple-combination (all3) benefit is concentrated in ever-smokers and minimal in never-smokers, consistent with the smoking × PD-L1 interaction.

analyses.result_summary: ORR in patients receiving all3 vs not, by smoking: current 23.6% vs 16.5% (n=1512), former 22.7% vs 16.5% (n=1565), never 19.4% vs 16.3% (n=139). Absolute benefit much larger in ever-smokers (+7 pp) than never-smokers (+3 pp). Pattern consistent with the smoking × PD-L1 mechanism.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 (top negative binary predictor) and feature_051 (ECOG-like) are INDEPENDENT (additive only) risk factors with no statistical interaction.
feature_013 has a similar negative effect within both adenocarcinoma and squamous histology subgroups (no interaction with histology).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef=+0.013, p=0.73. Main effects: feature_013 coef=-0.31, feature_051 coef=-0.38, both p<1e-30. Effects are additive only — no synergy or antagonism. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.
Adeno: ORR(f013+) 15.1% vs (f013-) 19.5%, p=9.3e-27. Squamous: 16.0% vs 19.9%, p=1.2e-8. Effect size and direction nearly identical across histology; hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Race (feature_123) and insurance (feature_005) jointly determine ORR through a non-additive interaction, reflecting health-disparity patterns rather than independent main effects.
Specifically, Asian patients on Medicaid have a substantially LOWER ORR than Asian patients with other insurance, even though Asian race is overall associated with above-average ORR.

analyses.result_summary: Race × insurance interaction terms include several significant pairs: White:Medicare coef=-0.40 (p=0.024), White:Private -0.46 (p=0.009), Black:Medicare -0.44 (p=0.030), Hispanic:Private -0.43 (p=0.029). LR-test of interaction is significant. Pattern suggests insurance-related ORR differences are not uniform across racial groups — supporting non-additive interaction as hypothesized.

Asian patients: ORR Medicaid 12.6% (n=388), Medicare 18.0% (n=1264), Private 19.2% (n=1206), Uninsured 17.4% (n=121). Asian-Medicaid vs other Asian: coef=-0.45, OR=0.64, p=0.005. Striking gap as hypothesized.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of high feature_051 (ECOG-like) on ORR is uniform across racial groups — i.e., ECOG penalty is not modified by race.
The negative effect of high feature_051 is uniform across insurance types — no insurance × ECOG interaction.
feature_011 (mets-like) does not interact with histology (effect is the same in adeno vs squamous).

analyses.result_summary: All race × feature_051 interactions p>0.45 (largest coef magnitude 0.11, all NS). Main feature_051 coef=-0.38, p<1e-7. ECOG penalty homogeneous across race as hypothesized.
All insurance × feature_051 interactions p>0.31 (largest coef magnitude 0.06, all NS). ECOG penalty homogeneous across insurance as hypothesized.
Interaction term coef=-0.002, p=0.74. Main effect of feature_011 coef=-0.038, p<1e-22. Feature_011 acts uniformly across histology as hypothesized.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_092 (PD-L1-like) interacts with feature_006 (a probable treatment-component) — its positive effect on ORR is restricted to patients receiving feature_006.
feature_092 does NOT interact significantly with histology (feature_043).
feature_099 (albumin-like) and feature_051 (ECOG-like) act additively on ORR — both prognostic but independent.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef=+0.594, p<1e-5. Main effect of feature_092 in feature_006-negative patients is essentially null; among feature_006-positive patients, feature_092 effect is strongly positive. Strong synergy as hypothesized.
Interaction coef=+0.011, p=0.93. PD-L1 effect homogeneous across histology as hypothesized.
Interaction coef=-0.013, p=0.73. Main effects: feature_099 +0.10 (p=0.004), feature_051 -0.33 (p=0.02). Effects are additive as hypothesized.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a comprehensive adjusted multivariable logistic regression including main effects of feature_051, feature_013, feature_067, feature_011, feature_006, feature_007, feature_039, feature_092, feature_099, feature_063, smoking, histology, race, insurance, and age, plus the key feature_006:feature_092, feature_007:feature_092, and feature_039:feature_092 interactions, the following will remain significant (p<0.01) with their hypothesized directions: feature_051 (-), feature_013 (-), feature_067 (-), feature_011 (-), feature_099 (+), feature_063 (-), and all three component:feature_092 interactions (+).

analyses.code: logit(y ~ feature_051 + feature_013 + feature_067 + feature_011 + feature_006 + feature_007 + feature_039 + feature_006:feature_092 + feature_007:feature_092 + feature_039:feature_092 + feature_092 + feature_099 + feature_063 + ...)
analyses.result_summary: Final adjusted coefficients (all p<0.001 unless noted): feature_051=-0.38, feature_013=-0.30, feature_067=-0.26, feature_011=-0.039, feature_099=+0.092, feature_063=-0.005, feature_006:feature_092=+0.60, feature_007:feature_092=+0.62, feature_039:feature_092=+0.59. The unconditional feature_092 main effect becomes negative (-0.60, p<0.001) reflecting that without any of f006/f007/f039, higher PD-L1 is non-beneficial (or slightly harmful). Smoking, histology, race, age and feature_044 (BMI-like) drop out as non-significant after adjustment. Hypothesis FULLY SUPPORTED.
